replies.msg:
請大家推上去置頂！

根據理大圍城逃出來嘅手足回憶，香港警察在理大曾經開槍打死佢嘅同伴，呢單嘢香港警察無報導，可以肯定中槍嘅女手足已經遇害
//
在理大被埋伏 - Ambushed at PolyU, acrylic painting 12"x16" by Ricker Choi

有位手足聯絡我, 希望我能夠用畫作記錄佢喺理大逃走時恐怖的一幕, 希望能夠對佢的心靈有所安慰。

A Hong Kong youth has contacted me, asking me to paint a scene when escaping from Polytechnic University as it was under siege by HK police. Hope this can have soul-healing effects.

//我係其中一個PolyU出得番黎嘅人，有一幕係我腦海入面揮之不去。

果陣係第一日被包圍既黃昏，我地一行三人嘗試離開poly校園。我地打算係山坡逃離ployu嘅時侯，我前面嘅女仔被黑警冇預警下喺正面開左一槍。我聽到佢大叫一聲，之後喺樓梯倒下。當時我好驚，即刻轉身跑走冇救番佢入polyu。之後都唔見有救護車進入校園，更加唔知果個女仔嘅下落。好愧疚當時轉身跑番入poly campus咩都做唔到。

輾轉之下流亡到海外，但不論身處何方呢一幕都經常係腦海浮起什致會發惡夢同食唔落飯。過左好耐先認識到有樣野叫做創傷後壓力症(PTSD)。

光復香港嘅路好漫長，一齊飲多d水，讀多d書做社會嘅棟樑。//

有關創傷後壓力症(PTSD): 心之流 FLOW Tool (Hong Kong)
https://hku.au1.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_6zEJ0R1E6aLqyJn

----------- English translation by Ricker

//I am one of those who was able to escape from Polytechnical University of Hong Kong when it was under siege by police. There is one moment that I can never forget.

It was the first evening when it was under siege. Me and two others were trying to leave the campus. While we were on our escape route at a hillside, the girl in front of me, without warning, was fired upon by a police in ambush. I heard a loud scream, and then she fell down the staircase. I was very scared, and I immediately turned around and ran, without attempting to save her. I waited but didn’t see any ambulance coming into the campus, and I had no idea the fate of the girl being shot. I was very guilty for running away and didn’t do anything to help save her.

Now I am in exile, but no matter where I am this moment keeps replaying in my head. I keep having nightmare and unable to eat. After a long while only then I realize these are symptoms of Post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD).

Liberation of Hong Kong is a long road. Drink more water! Study hard! And contribute to our society!//

About PTSD: https://hku.au1.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_6zEJ0R1E6aLqyJn
//
Source：
https://www.facebook.com/656681356/posts/10158863310156357/?d=n

//I am one of those who was able to escape from Polytechnical University of Hong Kong when it was under siege by police. There is one moment that I can never forget.

It was the first evening when it was under siege. Me and two others were trying to leave the campus. While we were on our escape route at a hillside, the girl in front of me, without warning, was fired upon by a police in ambush. I heard a loud scream, and then she fell down the staircase. I was very scared, and I immediately turned around and ran, without attempting to save her. I waited but didn’t see any ambulance coming into the campus, and I had no idea the fate of the girl being shot. I was very guilty for running away and didn’t do anything to help save her.

Now I am in exile, but no matter where I am this moment keeps replaying in my head. I keep having nightmare and unable to eat. After a long while only then I realize these are symptoms of Post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD).

Liberation of Hong Kong is a long road. Drink more water! Study hard! And contribute to our society!//
